programme.title: Escape to the Country

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Mrs Featherstone, aka the Black Widow, decides the time has come to learn to drive - if only she can find an instructor prepared to give her more than one lesson. Kath organises a bring and buy sale for charity, but Eric is less than happy when he discovers she is planning to put his favourite clothes on her stall. Meanwhile, romance seems to be blossoming for Madge and Gastric, and Willis finally gets away from his mother to go on a date with Ruby. David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy and Johnny Vegas star(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Keanu becomes increasingly frustrated with Mel's involvement and admits his concerns to Lisa. Mel's plan begins to unravel and she goes to desperate lengths to ensure she still gets what she wants. Ben lets slip to Karen that Louise and Keanu are moving to Portugal, leaving her devastated. Adam shows his true colours, unaware Mariam is listening to every word he says, leading Habiba and Iqra to hatch a plan to cheer their grandmother up. A furious Tiffany confronts Dotty over her actions(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Jo Brand assumes the role of host as panellists poke fun at the week's events. Comedian Zoe Lyons and columnist, literary editor and drama critic Fintan O'Toole bolster the efforts of regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Actors and comedians Liz Carr and Miles Jupp, actor and writer Samson Kayo and broadcaster Anneka Rice.join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As ever, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: She's delighted family audiences as Mary Poppins and The Sound of Music's Maria, yet retained the power to shock by appearing in the likes of 10 and A Touch of Adultery. Now singer and actress Julie Andrews is taking stock of her life in Home Work: A Memoir of My Hollywood Years, a follow-up to her critically acclaimed memoir, Home. Also appearing are Hollywood stars Reese Witherspoon and Jennifer Aniston, both starring in comedy drama series The Morning Show, and Ian McKellen, who is playing a conman in drama thriller The Good Liar. Plus, Dua Lipa performs her new single Don't Start Now(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Claire and Fleabag return to their father's home for an annual family gathering to mark the anniversary of their mother's death - but this time there is an unwanted guest. Fleabag gets into a confrontation with her godmother, who reveals her plans for a sexually charged art exhibition. Comedy, written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: For this week's task, the candidates head to the historic university cities of Oxford and Cambridge to procure nine items that are associated with both places. The added difficulty for the candidates is that they are given no clues as to which of the cities the various items are located in. In Oxford, timewasting leads to a baffling back-and-forth for one team, while in Cambridge, the other team gamble with their negotiations. Back in the boardroom, one of the contestants leaves the competition(n)

programme.title: Breakfast

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Four workmates at the Bruichladdich whisky distillery on Islay compete for the Flour Power Trophy. Cask master Jay, housekeeper Katie and tour guide Dave are joined by client manager Mary. Local head chef Stephen Crichton joins series judge Nicola Reith to decide on the winner, with plenty of fighting 'spirit' needed from the four whisky wizards as they create their signature bakes(n)

programme.title: Football Focus

programme.title: International Rugby League

programme.title: Money for Nothing

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at life on each of the seven continents, revealing how each distinct land mass has shaped the creatures found there and the many challenges faced by animals in a modern world dominated by humans. He begins with Antarctica, where Weddell seals grind back the ice with their teeth, colourful starfish carpet the seabed, huge colonies of king penguins crowd any ice-free land, and four-ton elephant seals fight for territory on the beach. However, the ocean here is warming - and with that comes an uncertain future(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys ushers comedian Joe Pasquale, Newsround presenter Ricky Boleto, singer Toyah Willcox and Olympic sprinter Jeanette Kwakye to the black chair to be put to the test on their chosen specialist subjects, which are werewolf films, number one singles of the 1990s, Boudica, and Disney animation respectively. The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions in a bid to carry off the commemorative trophy(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present the seventh live episode of this year's series, as the 10 remaining celebrity couples take to the dancefloor, hoping to impress the judges and the viewers at home. Motsi Mabuse, Craig Revel Horwood, Shirley Ballas and Bruno Tonioli will score the performances out of 10 before the voting lines open - giving viewers at home the chance to have their say and determine who will be leaving the competition(n)

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the fourth episode, Hannah and Geoff, a couple from London, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jade is faced with a dilemma after a chance encounter leaves her feeling heartbroken. Meanwhile, Ethan regrets his decisions when Effie is rushed into hospital and he has to open up to Theo about the lies he told so she would not be refused the drug trial. Elsewhere, Danny's impromptu visit to his childhood home does not go to plan(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Aston Villa v Liverpool at Villa Park and Manchester City v Southampton at the Etihad Stadium. This was the first meeting between the Villans and Reds since Valentine's Day in 2016, when Jurgen Klopp's side hammered their hosts 6-0. Pep Guardiola's City also scored six against the Saints in last season's corresponding fixture, on their way to the Premier League title. Plus, action from Arsenal v Wolverhampton Wanderers at the Emirates Stadium, Brighton & Hove Albion v Norwich City at the Amex Stadium, Bournemouth v Manchester United at the Vitality Stadium, Sheffield United v Burnley at Bramall Lane, Watford v Chelsea at Vicarage Road, and West Ham United v Newcastle United at London Stadium(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game - Arizona Cardinals v San Francisco 49ers at State Farm Stadium. Plus, a look to tomorrow's clash at Wembley Stadium between Jacksonville Jaguars and Houston Texans(n)

programme.title: Disturbia (2007)
programme.desc: A troubled teenager is sentenced to three months under house arrest for attacking a teacher, and passes the time by spying on his neighbours. However, one day he notices something suspicious and becomes convinced one of them is a serial killer. Thriller, starring Shia LaBeouf, Carrie-Anne Moss, David Morse and Sarah Roemer(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Aston Villa v Liverpool at Villa Park and Manchester City v Southampton at the Etihad Stadium. This was the first meeting between the Villans and Reds since Valentine's Day in 2016, when Jurgen Klopp's side hammered their hosts 6-0. Pep Guardiola's City also scored six against the Saints in last season's corresponding fixture, on their way to the Premier League title. Plus, action from Arsenal v Wolverhampton Wanderers at the Emirates Stadium, Brighton & Hove Albion v Norwich City at the Amex Stadium, Bournemouth v Manchester United at the Vitality Stadium, Sheffield United v Burnley at Bramall Lane, Watford v Chelsea at Vicarage Road, and West Ham United v Newcastle United at London Stadium(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Sunday Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region(n)

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Topical debate covering religious, moral and ethical issues, with guest panellists and contributions from viewers(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Having moved to the UK from Australia almost 50 years ago, Simon MacLean longs to return, and hopes a trial week in Perth will persuade fiancee Jane(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning and the team are at Wetherby racecourse, where the reds and blues battle it out with experts Charles Hanson and Kate Bliss before they go to auction(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Singer Russell Watson tells Aled Jones how a near-death experience transformed his Christian faith, while the pair sing hymns including In Christ Alone and Ave Maria(n)

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin is in Pembrokeshire with a couple who want not only to buy their first house together, but also need accommodation for holidaymakers and a horse(n)

programme.title: War Horse (2011)
programme.desc: A farmer's son forms a close bond with a horse he trains, but is forced to give the animal up when it is sold to the British army during the First World War. The horse passes through various owners on both sides as he travels across the battlefields of France, while the boy refuses to give up on being reunited. Steven Spielberg's drama based on Michael Morpurgo's book, starring Jeremy Irvine, Tom Hiddleston and Benedict Cumberbatch(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team looks at all things autumnal, with Ellie Harrison on Alderney where autumn storms have washed up seaweed that is providing a seasonal feast for an unexpected animal. John Craven takes a walk in the footsteps of John Keats to explore the landscape that inspired his famous poem To Autumn, while Anita Rani is on the farm of Michelin-starred chef Tommy Banks to get some hints on pickling and preserving autumn produce(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough turns his attention to life in Asia, the planet's most varied and extreme continent, which stretches from the Arctic Circle to the equator. Walruses gather in huge numbers in the frozen north and brown bears roam around remote Russian volcanoes, while the deep jungle provides sanctuary for the last few Sumatran rhino. There is also a glimpse of rarely seen creatures, from yeti-like golden snub-nosed monkeys in the mountain forests of China to a bizarre predator in the baking deserts of Iran(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes, with the two couples with the lowest combined scores taking part in a dance-off to decide who will be next to leave the contest. With music by Emeli Sande, who performs her single Shine, and an Elton John-inspired group dance(n)

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Orphan Lyra Belacqua's world is turned upside-down by her long-absent uncle's return from the North, while the glamorous Mrs Coulter visits Jordan College with a proposition. Jack Thorne's adaptation of the first of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen, Ruth Wilson, James McAvoy, Clarke Peters and Anne-Marie Duff(n)

programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: Paris falls to the Germans with not a single shot fired and Webster begins a campaign of resistance under the nose of the Nazis at the American hospital, while in Berlin, Mr and Mrs Rossler are arrested for neglecting their patriotic duties. Harry has returned home to Manchester with one of the Senegalese troops who stayed by his side to transport soldiers off Dunkirk beach, and is dumbfounded to learn that a newly formed special operations unit is interested in having him on board. Lois finds herself growing closer to Vernon(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from today's Premier League fixtures - Crystal Palace v Leicester City at Selhurst Park and Everton v Tottenham Hotspur Goodison Park. Palace and Leicester were both looking to build on their strong starts to the campaign, while Everton and Spurs were looking to improve on some disappointing results in the early stages of the season. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee and Lucy take the children out trick or treating for Halloween, but the night soon turns disastrous for Lee when he loses his mobile on the porch of a spooky Victorian house. After returning to the property to retrieve his phone, Lee discovers that the building is full of secrets that would be far better left undisturbed(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades sifts through more junk, taking away a collection of carpet off-cuts, a pair of tired chairs and a bicycle wheel, hoping he and two others can inject new life into them(n)

programme.title: Countryfile Autumn Diaries
programme.desc: Paul Martin investigates an invasive aquatic plant that is clogging up waterways and putting wildlife at risk, and Keeley Donovan visits Gloucester's Woodchester Mansion, home to the largest roost of endangered bats in the country(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Mary Berry comes to tea with Monty Don and, as well has giving her a tour of Longmeadow, he offers his advice on her new garden. Frances Tophill meets gardeners on an allotment in Birmingham and harvests some unusual vegetables, while Adam Frost and Arit Anderson join forces to transform a front garden in Hampshire into a haven for the owners as well as wildlife. In Nottinghamshire, the programme meets an enthusiast who has amassed a collection of over 100 varieties of rhubarb and in Scotland a herb grower with over 600 different types(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests assess the state of play regarding Brexit as they discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Yes Chef
programme.desc: The weekly final sees chefs Galton Blackiston, Frances Atkins, Rupert Rowley and Theo Randall pair up with their chosen home cook partners and go head to head in the ultimate pro-am cooking competition. And to pile on the pressure, three-star Michelin chef Pierre Koffmann judges their final dishes. Culinary challenge, hosted by Sheree Murphy(n)

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: After the detectives' hard work, a person visits the home of a relative she didn't know existed, and who surprisingly lives just 10 minutes' from her own house. On another case, the hunters experience a strange sense of deja vu when they trace heirs to the estate of a woman they have met before, and their search uncovers the remarkable history of Thames bargemen. Plus, details of more unclaimed estates are divulged(n)

programme.title: Streetdance (2010)
programme.desc: A street dance crew trains for a national competition, only to find themselves forced to share rehearsal rooms with a ballet company. While the two groups take an instant dislike to one another, the conflict fades over time and they make plans to combine their different styles into a new form of modern dance. Drama, with Nichola Burley, Richard Winsor and Charlotte Rampling(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts as children's TV presenters Hayley Hassall, Martin Dougan, Radzi Chinyanganya, Ayshah Tull and Katie Thistleton compete for charity(n)

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: In Exeter, former air traffic controller Paul Johnson takes on behavioural safety consultant Keith Jackson in the antiques challenge. Along the way presenter Paul Martin teaches them the tricks of the trade and the dos and don'ts of how to be an antique dealer(n)

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Zoe Ball and Rylan Clark-Neal present analysis of the pro-celebrity couples' progress ahead of tomorrow night's show. Celebrity super fans discuss all things ballroom, Gethin Jones catches up with the couples backstage at this week's Strictly rehearsals, and there's a live music performance in the studio(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Radio host Dev Griffin, comedian Alex Horne, presenter June Sarpong and the lovely Debbie McGee go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games, vying to be crowned weekly champion(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys grills four more contenders on their chosen subjects, which are Greek mythology, Manchester City FC, American novelist Harper Lee and EF Benson's Mapp and Lucia novels. This is followed by a two-minute general knowledge round(n)

programme.title: Autumnwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Michaela Strachan, Gillian Burke and Iolo Williams end their week-long adventure in the Cairngorms, looking at the autumns of the future and discovering encouraging news about Scottish wildcats. Gillian shares her amazing encounter with an 8ft-long blue shark, and there's an update on Cairngorms Connect, Britain's Great Landscape restoration project(n)

programme.title: The Name of the Rose
programme.desc: William and Adso find Berengar dead, and when Severinus discovers William's missing lenses in the late man's habit, William is sure they have the identity of Adso's attacker. The Franciscan and Papal envoys arrive for the conference, but bring news the Pope is sending Bernard Gui, the grand inquisitor, as his envoy. William and Adso continue their search for a secret chamber, the Finis Africae, and find various mechanisms to deter intruders. Adso is distracted by the presence of the strange Occitan girl in the forest, who is also the object of desire of the strange and demented Salvatore(n)

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Giles Coren and Monica Galetti work in Ireland's Ashford Castle. Set in 350 acres, it was founded 800 years ago by the Anglo-Norman De Burgo family, and much later became the family home of the Guinness family. With the help of hotel historian, Fintan, Giles discovers that the castle has had a rollercoaster journey through the centuries, sporadically reaching moments of real jeopardy, only to be saved by a turn of events or new owners. Giles and Monica soon learn that the secret of the castle's success is the people who work here and the hotel's strong, long-running community ties(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Eshaan Akbar, Angela Barnes, Ed Gamble, Rhys James and Felicity Ward and regular panellist Hugh Dennis. As usual, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Extended edition of the music programme, featuring Birmingham rapper and freestyle queen Lady Leshurr as co-host. Manchester's finest Elbow perform a track from their eighth album Giants of All Sizes, Kasabian's Serge Pizzorno presents his solo project the SLP, which combines rock guitars and dance music and folk-rock singer Michael Kiwanuka offers music from his self-titled third album. Completing the line-up are two acts shortlisted for the 2019 Mercury Prize - north London rapper Little Simz and rapper Slowthai, making his debut on the show. Jools also finds time to chat to comedian James Acaster(n)

programme.title: Half of a Yellow Sun (2013)
programme.desc: Two sisters return to their home in newly-liberated Nigeria in the 1960s after going to university in England. Their professional and romantic lives drive them in different directions and a betrayal pushes them further apart - but both are soon drawn into the civil war sweeping the country. Drama based on Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie's novel, starring Thandie Newton, Anika Noni Rose and Chiwetel Ejiofor(n)

programme.title: Catching Britain's Killers: The Crimes That Changed Us
programme.desc: The stories of murder investigations and their extraordinary consequences, which overturned laws, transformed police interrogation and revolutionised forensic detection. The second episode focuses on the story of the murder of Julie Hogg, a single mother from the small town of Billingham in Teesside. The programme looks back at Julie's mother Ann's campaign to challenge the 800-year-old law on Double Jeopardy, after a jury failed to convict Julie's former boyfriend Billy Dunlop. As this episode traces the twists and turns of Ann's campaign, the story of other cases that benefited from the legal changes are also explored(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Live International Rugby League
programme.desc: New Zealand v Great Britain (Kick-off 4.00am). Mark Chapman presents coverage of the second Test in a four-match tour for the Lions, which takes place at Eden Park in Auckland. With commentary by Dave Woods and Brian Noble(n)

programme.title: Top Class
programme.desc: Two teams take on the challenge of answering questions(n)

programme.title: Mustangs FC
programme.desc: When the Wildcats bully Ruby, Anusha gets carried away with a plan to take action(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: It's Halloween, so there's a spooky feel to Blue Peter today(n)

programme.title: The Amelia Gething Complex
programme.desc: Amelia moves into a new apartment with her best friends, Vinny and Poppy(n)

programme.title: Strange Magic (2015)
programme.desc: A fairy living in an enchanted forest tries to rescue her sister from a monstrous creature. Meanwhile, the magical kingdom is thrown into chaos by a love potion, that results in a series of unexpected romances. Animated fantasy musical, with the voices of Evan Rachel Wood, Alan Cumming and Kristin Chenoweth(n)

programme.title: Robot Wars
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon present more battles in the arena. A team of engineering students has created a robot that is able to move easily in any direction, which they send into battle against a group of holiday camp staff and a father and son duo whose machine is equipped with huge piercing claws(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Daniel Brocklebank and Hayley Tamaddon search for antiques in East Anglia, taking a trip to Great Yarmouth's Pleasure Beach and discovering a scheme to rehouse unwanted dogs(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers prepare salmon pie with spinach and hollandaise, while their chocolate and cherry buns make a perfect centrepiece(n)

programme.title: Children in Need
programme.desc: Ten well-known actors come together to record an album for Children in Need. Cameras follow Helena Bonham Carter, Jim Broadbent, Olivia Colman, Shaun Dooley, Luke Evans, Suranne Jones , Adrian Lester, Himesh Patel, David Tennant and Jodie Whittaker over 12 weeks in the summer of 2019 as they rehearse songs for their album, Got It Covered. Each actor must choose a song that has special meaning for them and must make their cover version their own. The participants also visit some charity projects that Children in Need supports in order to help raise funds and awareness(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: The judges return to Cardiff to give the winning homes from each category a second look, getting a fresh insight into each before deciding which one they like the most. Narrated by Marcus Garve(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Extraordinary Homes
programme.desc: Architect Piers Taylor and property enthusiast Caroline Quentin explore unusual homes built in mountainous regions. They begin in California, viewing a property built from the wings and tail fins of a Boeing 747. In Arizona, the pair stay in a modern house with an innovative take on an ancient technique of absorbing the heat during the day and releasing it at night. In New Zealand, Caroline and Piers view a house camouflaged using cedar cladding, while their last stop takes them to an hexagonal alpine chalet with a steel chimney core that anchors it to the mountain(n)

programme.title: Queen of Katwe (2016)
programme.desc: The life of a poor Ugandan youngster is changed for ever when she is introduced to the game of chess. Drama, starring David Oyelowo, Lupita Nyong'o and Madina Nalwanga(n)

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: Aircraft carrier HMS Queen Elizabeth sets out on its first transatlantic crossing, a four-month deployment on the high seas which has the primary purpose of putting the top secret F-35B Lightning Stealth Fighter to the test. Within hours of leaving Portsmouth, the vessel encounters a Russian warship and needs to take immediate evasive action, and the crew has to deal with floods and dangerous fuel leaks on their way to Florida(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Corporal Jones misplaces £500 donated by local shopkeepers to provide a new canteen for servicemen, so the platoon tries to help him remember what he did with it. Comedy first broadcast in 1972, starring Clive Dunn, John Le Mesurier and Arthur Lowe(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Author Helen Macdonald returns to the world of the goshawk, a bird she first wrote about in her best-selling 2014 memoir H Is for Hawk, a book originally written to help get over the grief of losing her father. Here she trains a new bird and follows a wild goshawk family at the nest, and getting closer than ever before to these fiery-eyed birds of prey(n)

programme.title: Great Australian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores New South Wales by train, travelling 570 miles from Sydney to the mining city of Broken Hill. He explores the origins of Sydney as a penal colony, visiting the Hyde Park barracks where many of the first convicts were housed. He also tours the city's iconic opera house, and volunteers for the lifesaving club at Bondi Beach. In the Blue Mountains, he learns about people's relationship to the land from his indigenous guide, before reaching Katoomba, where he rides the steepest railway in the world(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig delves into a bit of 'quintessential' QI, with panellists Alan Davies, Cariad Lloyd, Holly Walsh and Josh Widdicombe at the brunt of her quizzing. As usual, the questions are so difficult points are instead awarded for the answers the host finds most interesting(n)

programme.title: Pose
programme.desc: Elektra, now working part-time as a dominatrix, finds herself in trouble, so she seeks help from her community. Angel cannot let go of her modelling ambitions - and her long-awaited lucky break may just be around the corner. US drama, starring Dominique Jackson and Angel Bismark(n)

programme.title: Pose
programme.desc: The first official Masters of Ceremony Council meeting takes place in a diner amid fierce disagreements about what new categories should be added. But the community soon has more to worry about when one of the members is brutally attacked(n)

programme.title: Election (1999)
programme.desc: A teacher develops an irrational dislike for an overambitious student and plans to sabotage her campaign for school president. He persuades a dim-witted athlete to stand, in the hope he will pip the high-achieving front-runner to the post - but the intervention of another student throws the plan into disarray. Satirical comedy, starring Matthew Broderick, Reese Witherspoon, Chris Klein and Jessica Campbell(n)

programme.title: The British Guide to Showing Off (2011)
programme.desc: Part-animated documentary about Andrew Logan's Alternative Miss World show, which was first launched in 1972 and has since gone through 12 revivals. The film celebrates some of the memorable and outlandish contestants to have taken part over the past 40 years. Featuring appearances from Brian Eno, Richard O'Brien, Grayson Perry and Zandra Rhodes(n)

programme.title: The Name of the Rose
programme.desc: William and Adso find Berengar dead, and when Severinus discovers William's missing lenses in the late man's habit, William is sure they have the identity of Adso's attacker. The Franciscan and Papal envoys arrive for the conference, but bring news the Pope is sending Bernard Gui, the grand inquisitor, as his envoy. William and Adso continue their search for a secret chamber, the Finis Africae, and find various mechanisms to deter intruders. Adso is distracted by the presence of the strange Occitan girl in the forest, who is also the object of desire of the strange and demented Salvatore(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Great Chelsea Garden Challenge
programme.desc: Joe Swift takes the remaining designers to the grounds of Sudeley Castle in Gloucestershire, where they have to create formal gardens to impress RHS judge James Alexander Sinclair and award-winning garden designer Ann-Marie Powell. They have a mere four days to showcase their design skills and have to include a two metre high piece of topiary they shaped from yew hedge(n)

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Gardens
programme.desc: Monty Don continues his horticultural tour with a visit to the dreamy climes of Australia and New Zealand. Visiting the renowned Botany Bay, Monty investigates the effect imported plant-life, favoured by colonial settlers, has had on the natural beauty of New South Wales. In New Zealand, the green-fingered presenter goes in search of the wild heart of the country, revelling in a lush rainforest conservatory made entirely of native plants(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In the fifth annual event, the team heads out across the UK to meet inspirational youngsters who have been helped by the charity. Matt Baker takes on a challenge laid down by 14-year-old Ethan, who was born with cerebral palsy, while Ellie Harrison and Steve Brown lead the biggest ramble of all along the city of Worcester's riverside. John Craven leads a walk through the grounds of Perthshire's Scone Palace, Anita Rani is on Cornwall's north coast, Adam Henson is in Glenariff Forest Park in Co Antrim, and Tom Heap is in south Wales(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at television appearances made over the years by American film actor Tony Curtis, capturing the milestones and highlights of his life and career, which led to him becoming considered a Hollywood star. Narrated by Sylvia Syms(n)

programme.title: The Vikings (1958)
programme.desc: A Norse warrior becomes embroiled in a feud with a former slave over the succession to the Northumbrian throne, unaware his arch-enemy is really his half-brother. Matters come to a head when they both set their sights on a Welsh princess, leading to a confrontation - but the situation is further complicated by the interference of England's sadistic king. Adventure, with Kirk Douglas, Tony Curtis, Janet Leigh, Ernest Borgnine and James Donald(n)

programme.title: NFL Live
programme.desc: Jacksonville Jaguars v Houston Texans (Kick-off 2.30pm). Nat Coombs presents coverage of the fourth and final International Series match of the campaign, as the AFC South sides meet at Wembley Stadium. The match is set to feature two of the most promising young quarterbacks in the NFL in the form of the Jaguars' Gardner Minshew II and Deshuan Watson of the Texans. With analysis from Osi Umenyiora, Jason Bell and Mike Carlson(n)

programme.title: Africa with Ade Adepitan
programme.desc: The presenter concludes his journey by exploring southern Africa. In Mozambique, he discovers that a declining tourist trade has benefited wildlife and explores local attitudes to disability. Moving on to South Africa, he investigates how much life has changed in the 25 years since the downfall of apartheid. His journey ends in Zimbabwe, a country that is still struggling economically despite having vast quantities of gold(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Deborah Meaden, Peter Jones, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and Jenny Campbell consider the profit-making potential of a crowdfunded range of personalised luxury bags, a range of free-from, allergy-friendly frozen curry sauces and a thick-yarn crafting business(n)

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: The captain of HMS Queen Elizabeth deliberately seeks out the worst possible weather so he can test how the F35 jets cope under extreme conditions - and gets his wish when the aircraft carrier ends up in the path of a hurricane. After consulting with his officers, he resolves to brave the elements and test the jets to the brink of destruction. The crew also faces the challenge of refueling the vessel at sea - one of the hardest tasks outside of going into battle(n)

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The explorer travels from Texas to southern Mexico, meeting a woman who risks her life to take supplies to migrants stranded on the Mexican side of the border in Reynosa, one of the country's most violent cities, which is dominated by a powerful criminal organisation. He also ventures into the rainforest to explore the Maya city of Yaxchilan, and meets the modern-day descendants of this civilisation, who are increasingly marginalised and treated as second-class citizens(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Sarah Millican hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, west London, treating the audience to her own brand of humour, before introducing routines by camp comic Joe Lycett and award-winning funnyman Russell Kane(n)

programme.title: Silence (2016)
programme.desc: In the 17th century, two Jesuit priests travel from Portugal to Edo-era Japan to locate their missing mentor and spread Catholic Christianity. Martin Scorsese's historical drama, starring Andrew Garfield, Adam Driver, Tadanobu Asano, Ciaran Hinds and Liam Neeson(n)

programme.title: World News Today
programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Simon Mayo and Peter Powell introduce the edition first broadcast on September 15, featuring Bros, the Commodores, the Proclaimers, the Pasadenas, Coldcut featuring Junior Reid, Salt-n-Pepa, Jason Donovan, the Hollies, Phil Collins and Inner City(n)

programme.title: Exotic Pop at the BBC
programme.desc: Compilation of archive performances by international artists who found success in the UK charts, including Greek crooner Demis Roussos, French star Vanessa Paradis, Swedish singer Sylvia and Romanian pan-pipe player Gheorghe Zamfir(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell and Andy Crane introduce the edition first broadcast on September 22, featuring Pet Shop Boys, Womack & Womack, Bill Withers, Transvision Vamp, Whitney Houston, Bon Jovi, Rick Astley, the Hollies and the Pasadenas(n)

programme.title: Get Rich or Try Dying: Music's Mega Legacies
programme.desc: Death has its obvious downside, but in the world of pop, shuffling off the mortal coil can offer a unique business opportunity. Last year, the top five estates of the most cherished but deceased popstars earned $0.5billion. Ana Matronic embarks on a journey into the afterlife of pop, discovering the many ways pop stars earn a living when they're dead. Contributors including agents, publicists, producers and families reveal the dos and don'ts of keeping the dream alive(n)

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Steve Wright introduces an edition of the music show featuring the spookiest, scariest and most frightening clips from the pop archives involving witches, monsters, ghosts, the Addams Family and Dracula. Including performances by the Automatic, the Crazy World of Arthur Brown, Ray Parker Jr, Bobby 'Boris' Pickett and Michael Jackson(n)

programme.title: Rod Stewart at the BBC
programme.desc: A tribute to the singer's career with a selection of his performances and interviews at the BBC. Songs include Sailing, You're in My Heart, I Don't Want to Talk About It and Da Ya Think I'm Sexy? Plus, Handbags and Gladrags from Rod's set at Glastonbury in 2002, his cover of Dorothy Fields' classic I'm in the Mood for Love and a song from his Radio 2 concert in May 2013(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Simon Mayo and Peter Powell introduce the edition first broadcast on September 15, featuring Bros, the Commodores, the Proclaimers, the Pasadenas, Coldcut featuring Junior Reid, Salt-n-Pepa, Jason Donovan, the Hollies, Phil Collins and Inner City(n)

programme.title: Totally 60s Psychedelic Rock at the BBC
programme.desc: A compilation of the genre from programmes such as Colour Me Pop, How It Is, Top of the Pops and Once More with Felix. Performers include Status Quo, the Incredible String Band, Donovan, the Bonzo Dog Doo-Dah Band, Julie Driscoll with Brian Auger and the Trinity, the Moody Blues, the Crazy World of Arthur Brown, Joe Cocker, the Move, Procol Harum, Cream, Jimi Hendrix and the Who(n)

programme.title: Exotic Pop at the BBC
programme.desc: Compilation of archive performances by international artists who found success in the UK charts, including Greek crooner Demis Roussos, French star Vanessa Paradis, Swedish singer Sylvia and Romanian pan-pipe player Gheorghe Zamfir(n)

programme.title: Tones, Drones and Arpeggios: The Magic of Minimalism
programme.desc: Charles Hazlewood concludes his exploration of minimalism, classical music's last great 'movement', which climaxed in the late 1970s on America's east coast. In the second of two episodes, he explores how breakthrough techniques first explored on tape by Steve Reich were transposed for orchestral performance, and how Philip Glass' experiments with repetitive structures along with his innovative work in opera, revealed new possibilities for classical music. The programme includes excerpts from minimalist pieces, plus part of Mike Oldfield's minimalist-inspired Tubular Bells. Contributors include Jarvis Cocker(n)

programme.title: Nature's Wonderlands: Islands of Evolution
programme.desc: Richard Fortey investigates the development of species on Madagascar, an ancient island that has spawned some of the most extraordinary groups of plants and animals anywhere in the world. The palaeontologist uncovers the secrets of the evolution of creatures including indri lemurs, toxic frogs and cat-like giant mongooses called fossa, as well as oddities like the giraffe-necked weevil and the aye-aye(n)

programme.title: Wild China
programme.desc: Examining the heartland of the country, home to ancient structures such as the Great Wall and modern buildings like Beijing's Olympic Stadium, as well as animals including the giant panda and golden snub-nosed monkey. However, the region holds even deeper cultural significance as the land of origin of China's most populous ethnic group the Han people - whose 5,000-year-old traditions are increasingly under threat(n)

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Following the debacle resulting in Fouad's death, the team is taken off the investigation. Laure is increasingly concerned about Lebrion's interference, while Josephine realises she also has reasons not to trust Edelman. In French(n)

programme.title: Spiral
programme.desc: Roban falls into a trap set by Josephine and Edelman, while Laure and Gilou identify a suspect in the money-laundering operation, and Beckriche's loyalty is put to the test. In French(n)

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Field Marshal Haig plans a new offensive against the Germans, and Capt Blackadder develops a keen interest in art to avoid the bloodshed. However, his newly discovered talents prove so useful to Military Intelligence that he finds himself stuck in the middle of No Man's Land with only his sidekicks for company. Classic comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson, Tony Robinson and Hugh Laurie(n)

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Captain Blackadder is put on trial for killing and eating General Melchett's carrier pigeon. The only thing standing between him and death by firing squad is his defence counsel - who unfortunately turns out to be George. Rowan Atkinson stars in Ben Elton and Richard Curtis's comedy, with Tony Robinson and Stephen Fry(n)

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: A morale-boosting stage show offers Captain Blackadder a route out of the trenches, but he soon finds his plans heading for disaster when Gen Melchett falls for the leading lady - Lt George. First World War comedy, with Rowan Atkinson, Tony Robinson and Hugh Laurie(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Mike Read and Andy Crane present the edition first shown on September 8, featuring performances by Marc Almond, Gloria Estefan and Miami Sound Machine, Spagna, Anthrax, Bill Medley, the Proclaimers, Michael Jackson, Yello, Phil Collins and Jane Wiedlin(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Simon Mayo and Peter Powell introduce the edition first broadcast on September 15, featuring Bros, the Commodores, the Proclaimers, the Pasadenas, Coldcut featuring Junior Reid, Salt-n-Pepa, Jason Donovan, the Hollies, Phil Collins and Inner City(n)

programme.title: Get Rich or Try Dying: Music's Mega Legacies
programme.desc: Death has its obvious downside, but in the world of pop, shuffling off the mortal coil can offer a unique business opportunity. Last year, the top five estates of the most cherished but deceased popstars earned $0.5billion. Ana Matronic embarks on a journey into the afterlife of pop, discovering the many ways pop stars earn a living when they're dead. Contributors including agents, publicists, producers and families reveal the dos and don'ts of keeping the dream alive(n)

programme.title: Franco Building with Jonathan Meades
programme.desc: The writer considers the architectural legacy of Francisco Franco, arguing that it is not found in the official buildings he commissioned, but in the clash of building styles along Spain's coastline and the proliferation of skyscrapers in the beach resorts such as Benidorm, where the dictator was keen to promote mass tourism, leading to the creation of a new kind of city(n)

programme.title: For Folk's Sake: Morris Dancing and Me
programme.desc: Richard Macer infiltrates morris dancing's endangered world of bells, beer and beards, discovering that women may be the unlikely saviours of this ancient masculine tradition(n)

programme.title: Britain's Nuclear Bomb: The Inside Story
programme.desc: In 1957, Britain exploded its first megaton hydrogen bomb, code-named Operation Grapple X. This documentary looks at how Britain became a nuclear superpower, featuring footage of atomic bomb tests, and interviews with veterans and scientists who took part in the extraordinary atomic bomb programme(n)

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Appealing to both adults and children alike, Philip Pullman's books have sold more than 20 million copies and been translated into 40 languages, with his recent novel La Belle Sauvage becoming an instant best-seller. Alan Yentob spends time with the author in Oxford, discovering how the former teacher became an acclaimed writer of fantasy fiction - and an outspoken critic of organised religion(n)

programme.title: Stanley Kubrick: A Life in Pictures
programme.desc: Documentary chronicling the life of one of the 20th century's greatest movie directors, whose works included A Clockwork Orange and 2001: A Space Odyssey. Narrated by Tom Cruise, this revealing insight into the career and private life of the film-maker features tributes from his wife of 42 years Christiane and many well-known actors including Woody Allen and Jack Nicholson(n)

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: A guided tour by 16-year-old Syrian boy, Ahmed, through one of the biggest refugee camps in the world - Zaatari in Jordan. Ahmed is like any other boy his age, except he's had to leave everything behind, including his toys and technology, to move to the refugee camp. Life in Zaatari is nothing compared to home. When the Syrian civil war began in 2012 his family had to abandon their affluent lives and flee across the border to Zaatari camp with thousands of others for safety. Now living in a storage container for a home after his house was destroyed in the civil war, he has lived in Zaatari for three years, along with 80,000 other refugees, who can't leave the camp without permission. Despite their camp having everything they need, from shops to a hospital, school and football pitches, life is very different to how it was before. Ahmed doesn't let this affect his positivity though, and he finds new ways to have fun with his friends - even if it is in a desert sandstorm followed by flood(n)

programme.title: Gareth Edwards' Great Welsh Adventure
programme.desc: The rugby legend and wife Maureen travel along the Monmouthshire and Brecon canal, sampling the breath-taking views of the Brecon Beacons. Determined to show that he has mastered the art of narrow-boating, Gareth takes charge of the Bentley of Barges. As a reward for their endeavours, the pair discover how to make gin with ingredients found in the hedgerow. When the canal passes the Glanusk Estate, Gareth seizes the chance to enjoy a spot of fly fishing. Later, in Brecon, they enjoy the performance of a jazz band - a style of music with which the town is synonymous thanks to its annual festival(n)

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Mrs Featherstone, aka the Black Widow, decides the time has come to learn to drive - if only she can find an instructor prepared to give her more than one lesson. Meanwhile, romance seems to be blossoming for Madge and Gastric, and Willis finally gets away from his mother to go on a date with Ruby. David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy and Johnny Vegas star(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Keanu becomes increasingly frustrated with Mel's involvement and admits his concerns to Lisa. Mel's plan begins to unravel and she goes to desperate lengths to ensure she still gets what she wants. Ben lets slip to Karen that Louise and Keanu are moving to Portugal, leaving her devastated. Adam shows his true colours, unaware Mariam is listening to every word he says, leading Habiba and Iqra to hatch a plan to cheer their grandmother up. A furious Tiffany confronts Dotty over her actions(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Jo Brand assumes the role of host as panellists poke fun at the week's events. Comedian Zoe Lyons and columnist, literary editor and drama critic Fintan O'Toole bolster the efforts of regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton(n)

programme.title: Scrum V Rugby World Cup Special
The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: She's delighted family audiences as Mary Poppins and The Sound of Music's Maria, yet retained the power to shock by appearing in the likes of 10 and A Touch of Adultery. Now singer and actress Julie Andrews is taking stock of her life in Home Work: A Memoir of My Hollywood Years, a follow-up to her critically acclaimed memoir, Home. Also appearing are Hollywood stars Reese Witherspoon and Jennifer Aniston, both starring in comedy drama series The Morning Show, and Ian McKellen, who is playing a conman in drama thriller The Good Liar. Plus, Dua Lipa performs her new single Don't Start Now(n)

programme.title: Tourist Trap
programme.desc: The country is gripped by Banksy fever following the discovery of one of his artworks in Port Talbot, while the staff of Wow Wales! settle into their new premises. Return of the comedy about a tourism agency, starring Sally Phillips(n)

programme.title: Beauty Queen and Single
programme.desc: Miss UK Beauty first runner-up 2015 Alaw is seeking someone special. She's conscious her parents would love her to find a Welsh speaker as that is their family's first language. Standing at just 4ft 11ins, Alaw's got a tall boy on her wish-list. She admits she comes across as confident on the outside but is a bag of nerves on the inside - and that's exactly how she feels as the make-under begins. Like all the beauty queens, Alaw's supported by stylist Nathan who provides the voice of reason and a personal twist on a simple black dress bespoke to each girls(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Claire and Fleabag return to their father's home for an annual family gathering to mark the anniversary of their mother's death - but this time there is an unwanted guest. Fleabag gets into a confrontation with her godmother, who reveals her plans for a sexually charged art exhibition. Comedy, written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Gennaro Contaldo and Shivi Ramoutar, and special guest Michael Ball. Plus, archive clips featuring Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, and Nigel Slater(n)

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Four workmates at the Bruichladdich whisky distillery on Islay compete for the Flour Power Trophy. Cask master Jay, housekeeper Katie and tour guide Dave are joined by client manager Mary. Local head chef Stephen Crichton joins series judge Nicola Reith to decide on the winner, with plenty of fighting 'spirit' needed from the four whisky wizards as they create their signature bakes(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Aston Villa v Liverpool and Manchester City v Southampton in the Premier League(n)

programme.title: International Rugby League
programme.desc: New Zealand v Great Britain. Mark Chapman presents action from the second Test in a four-match tour for the Lions, which took place at Eden Park in Auckland. With commentary by Dave Woods and Brian Noble(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from a recycling centre in Witley, Surrey, with Kev Paxton and Anthony Devine helping her transform a cooker hood, a sofa and a collection of jars(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at life on each of the seven continents, revealing how each distinct land mass has shaped the creatures found there and the many challenges faced by animals in a modern world dominated by humans. He begins with Antarctica, where Weddell seals grind back the ice with their teeth, colourful starfish carpet the seabed, huge colonies of king penguins crowd any ice-free land, and four-ton elephant seals fight for territory on the beach. However, the ocean here is warming - and with that comes an uncertain future(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys ushers comedian Joe Pasquale, Newsround presenter Ricky Boleto, singer Toyah Willcox and Olympic sprinter Jeanette Kwakye to the black chair to be put to the test on their chosen specialist subjects, which are werewolf films, number one singles of the 1990s, Boudica, and Disney animation respectively. The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions in a bid to carry off the commemorative trophy(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special Halloween edition of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Richard Blackwood, Joe Swash, Sam Nixon, Mark Rhodes, Yvette Fielding, Timmy Mallett, Anne Diamond and Kate Lawler(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present the seventh live episode of this year's series, as the 10 remaining celebrity couples take to the dancefloor, hoping to impress the judges and the viewers at home. Motsi Mabuse, Craig Revel Horwood, Shirley Ballas and Bruno Tonioli will score the performances out of 10 before the voting lines open - giving viewers at home the chance to have their say and determine who will be leaving the competition(n)

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the fourth episode, Hannah and Geoff, a couple from London, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jade is faced with a dilemma after a chance encounter leaves her feeling heartbroken. Meanwhile, Ethan regrets his decisions when Effie is rushed into hospital and he has to open up to Theo about the lies he told so she would not be refused the drug trial. Elsewhere, Danny's impromptu visit to his childhood home does not go to plan(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Aston Villa v Liverpool at Villa Park and Manchester City v Southampton at the Etihad Stadium. This was the first meeting between the Villans and Reds since Valentine's Day in 2016, when Jurgen Klopp's side hammered their hosts 6-0. Pep Guardiola's City also scored six against the Saints in last season's corresponding fixture, on their way to the Premier League title. Plus, action from Arsenal v Wolverhampton Wanderers at the Emirates Stadium, Brighton & Hove Albion v Norwich City at the Amex Stadium, Bournemouth v Manchester United at the Vitality Stadium, Sheffield United v Burnley at Bramall Lane, Watford v Chelsea at Vicarage Road, and West Ham United v Newcastle United at London Stadium(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game - Arizona Cardinals v San Francisco 49ers at State Farm Stadium. Plus, a look to tomorrow's clash at Wembley Stadium between Jacksonville Jaguars and Houston Texans(n)

programme.title: Disturbia (2007)
programme.desc: A troubled teenager is sentenced to three months under house arrest for attacking a teacher, and passes the time by spying on his neighbours. However, one day he notices something suspicious and becomes convinced one of them is a serial killer. Thriller, starring Shia LaBeouf, Carrie-Anne Moss, David Morse and Sarah Roemer(n)

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Topical debate covering religious, moral and ethical issues, with guest panellists and contributions from viewers(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Having moved to the UK from Australia almost 50 years ago, Simon MacLean longs to return, and hopes a trial week in Perth will persuade fiancee Jane(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning and the team are at Wetherby racecourse, where the reds and blues battle it out with experts Charles Hanson and Kate Bliss before they go to auction(n)

programme.title: War Horse (2011)
programme.desc: A farmer's son forms a close bond with a horse he trains, but is forced to give the animal up when it is sold to the British army during the First World War. The horse passes through various owners on both sides as he travels across the battlefields of France, while the boy refuses to give up on being reunited. Steven Spielberg's drama based on Michael Morpurgo's book, starring Jeremy Irvine, Tom Hiddleston and Benedict Cumberbatch(n)

programme.title: Gareth Edwards' Great Welsh Adventure

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team looks at all things autumnal, with Ellie Harrison on Alderney where autumn storms have washed up seaweed that is providing a seasonal feast for an unexpected animal. John Craven takes a walk in the footsteps of John Keats to explore the landscape that inspired his famous poem To Autumn, while Anita Rani is on the farm of Michelin-starred chef Tommy Banks to get some hints on pickling and preserving autumn produce(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough turns his attention to life in Asia, the planet's most varied and extreme continent, which stretches from the Arctic Circle to the equator. Walruses gather in huge numbers in the frozen north and brown bears roam around remote Russian volcanoes, while the deep jungle provides sanctuary for the last few Sumatran rhino. There is also a glimpse of rarely seen creatures, from yeti-like golden snub-nosed monkeys in the mountain forests of China to a bizarre predator in the baking deserts of Iran(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes, with the two couples with the lowest combined scores taking part in a dance-off to decide who will be next to leave the contest. With music by Emeli Sande, who performs her single Shine, and an Elton John-inspired group dance(n)

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Orphan Lyra Belacqua's world is turned upside-down by her long-absent uncle's return from the North, while the glamorous Mrs Coulter visits Jordan College with a proposition. programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: Paris falls to the Germans with not a single shot fired and Webster begins a campaign of resistance under the nose of the Nazis at the American hospital, while in Berlin, Mr and Mrs Rossler are arrested for neglecting their patriotic duties. Harry has returned home to Manchester with one of the Senegalese troops who stayed by his side to transport soldiers off Dunkirk beach, and is dumbfounded to learn that a newly formed special operations unit is interested in having him on board. Lois finds herself growing closer to Vernon(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2

This was the first meeting between the Villans and Reds since Valentine's Day in 2016, when Jurgen Klopp's side hammered their hosts 6-0. Pep Guardiola's City also scored six against the Saints in last season's corresponding fixture, on their way to the Premier League title. Plus, action from Arsenal v Wolverhampton Wanderers at the Emirates Stadium, Brighton & Hove Albion v Norwich City at the Amex Stadium, Bournemouth v Manchester United at the Vitality Stadium, Sheffield United v Burnley at Bramall Lane, Watford v Chelsea at Vicarage Road, and West Ham United v Newcastle United at London Stadium(n)

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game - Arizona Cardinals v San Francisco 49ers at State Farm Stadium. Plus, a look to tomorrow's clash at Wembley Stadium between Jacksonville Jaguars and Houston Texans(n)

programme.title: Disturbia (2007)
programme.desc: A troubled teenager is sentenced to three months under house arrest for attacking a teacher, and passes the time by spying on his neighbours. However, one day he notices something suspicious and becomes convinced one of them is a serial killer. Thriller, starring Shia LaBeouf, Carrie-Anne Moss, David Morse and Sarah Roemer(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features(n)
