programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: Sunday Politics Scotland
programme.desc: Gordon Brewer and guests look at the week's events at Westminster and Holyrood(n)

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Topical debate covering religious, moral and ethical issues, with guest panellists and contributions from viewers(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Having moved to the UK from Australia almost 50 years ago, Simon MacLean longs to return, and hopes a trial week in Perth will persuade fiancee Jane(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning and the team are at Wetherby racecourse, where the reds and blues battle it out with experts Charles Hanson and Kate Bliss before they go to auction(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Singer Russell Watson tells Aled Jones how a near-death experience transformed his Christian faith, while the pair sing hymns including In Christ Alone and Ave Maria(n)

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin is in Pembrokeshire with a couple who want not only to buy their first house together, but also need accommodation for holidaymakers and a horse(n)

programme.title: War Horse (2011)
programme.desc: A farmer's son forms a close bond with a horse he trains, but is forced to give the animal up when it is sold to the British army during the First World War. The horse passes through various owners on both sides as he travels across the battlefields of France, while the boy refuses to give up on being reunited. Steven Spielberg's drama based on Michael Morpurgo's book, starring Jeremy Irvine, Tom Hiddleston and Benedict Cumberbatch(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team looks at all things autumnal, with Ellie Harrison on Alderney where autumn storms have washed up seaweed that is providing a seasonal feast for an unexpected animal. John Craven takes a walk in the footsteps of John Keats to explore the landscape that inspired his famous poem To Autumn, while Anita Rani is on the farm of Michelin-starred chef Tommy Banks to get some hints on pickling and preserving autumn produce(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough turns his attention to life in Asia, the planet's most varied and extreme continent, which stretches from the Arctic Circle to the equator. Walruses gather in huge numbers in the frozen north and brown bears roam around remote Russian volcanoes, while the deep jungle provides sanctuary for the last few Sumatran rhino. There is also a glimpse of rarely seen creatures, from yeti-like golden snub-nosed monkeys in the mountain forests of China to a bizarre predator in the baking deserts of Iran(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman announce the results of the judges' scores and viewers' votes, with the two couples with the lowest combined scores taking part in a dance-off to decide who will be next to leave the contest. With music by Emeli Sande, who performs her single Shine, and an Elton John-inspired group dance(n)

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Orphan Lyra Belacqua's world is turned upside-down by her long-absent uncle's return from the North, while the glamorous Mrs Coulter visits Jordan College with a proposition. Jack Thorne's adaptation of the first of Philip Pullman's fantasy adventure novel series, starring Dafne Keen, Ruth Wilson, James McAvoy, Clarke Peters and Anne-Marie Duff(n)

programme.title: World on Fire
programme.desc: Paris falls to the Germans with not a single shot fired and Webster begins a campaign of resistance under the nose of the Nazis at the American hospital, while in Berlin, Mr and Mrs Rossler are arrested for neglecting their patriotic duties. Harry has returned home to Manchester with one of the Senegalese troops who stayed by his side to transport soldiers off Dunkirk beach, and is dumbfounded to learn that a newly formed special operations unit is interested in having him on board. Lois finds herself growing closer to Vernon(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from today's Premier League fixtures - Crystal Palace v Leicester City at Selhurst Park and Everton v Tottenham Hotspur Goodison Park. Palace and Leicester were both looking to build on their strong starts to the campaign, while Everton and Spurs were looking to improve on some disappointing results in the early stages of the season. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's top-flight goals(n)

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Jonathan Sutherland is joined by Michael Stewart and Steven Thompson to present action from the weekend's Premiership fixtures, which were Aberdeen v Kilmarnock at Pittodrie, Hamilton Academical v Ross County at Fountain of Youth Stadium and Motherwell v Livingston at Fir Park. Plus, reaction to the Betfred Cup semi-finals(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Countryfile Autumn Diaries
programme.desc: John Craven explores the stunning countryside around the historic city of Oxford. Paul Martin learns how Queen guitarist Brian May is playing his part in restoring ancient woodland in Dorset, while Margherita Taylor discovers how one of the UK's most scenic autumnal landscapes could be at risk(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts examines a lower ground flat in Pimlico, central London. Martel Maxwell is in Ipswich, Suffolk, and Huddersfield, West Yorkshire, visiting properties bought at auction and both in need of renovation(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: An instance when insurers found themselves dealing with phantom passengers in a two-car shunt. It transpired that opportunists were attempting to make personal injury claims for people that weren't even in the car. Presented by Naga Munchetty(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A couple restoring a house, who had earlier disagreed about installing CCTV, but later found the cameras proved their worth after an intruder invaded their dream home. Plus, the man robbing a pizza takeaway who got a taste of his own medicine when another thief took his bike. Presented by Dom Littlewood(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Richard Madley and Kate Bliss help two teams competing in Builth Wells, Powys. Presenter Charlie Ross monitors proceedings and also learns about Wales' washing machine industry(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Becky and Daniel's relationship hits a rocky patch, while Karen shares her sadness over Immie, and Jimmi confides in Emma about Daniel. The troops rally to help Jimmi and Al get the Icon shipshape(n)

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Operations manager Kate faces her first challenge, taking on money blogger Jordon on his fourth appearance. She's also up against veteran Andrew, a retired police officer marking his seventh time in the Head Hunter position. As usual, contestants choose between working together to combine their knowledge or going it alone to claim the prize for themselves. Quiz show, hosted by Rob Beckett(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin heads to the East Midlands to help a couple find a comfortable country home that brings them closer to family for £450,000. While in the region, Jonnie visits the Leicester Line of the Grand Union canal, a stretch where narrow boats have to navigate a total of 59 locks. He tries his hand at lock keeper's duties, including operating the floodgates(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore scours Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey for unloved items she can transform. She then turns to upholsterer extraordinaire Sharon O'Connor to make-over a tired armchair, while clockmaker Paul Parry gets creative with a collection of circuit boards. For her own project, Sarah attempts to transform a rusty metal table frame. Narrated by Arthur Smith(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Anita Manning and Paul Laidlaw point the classic car to an auction in Newcastle with only a few pounds separating them. A flurry of big bids makes for an exciting last auction for the pair(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic boxing bronze medallist Anthony Ogogo, five-time Paralympic champion Hannah Cockroft, former England rugby union player Ugo Monye and Formula One driver Jolyon Palmer join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Mrs Featherstone, aka the Black Widow, decides the time has come to learn to drive - if only she can find an instructor prepared to give her more than one lesson. Kath organises a bring and buy sale for charity, but Eric is less than happy when he discovers she is planning to put his favourite clothes on her stall. Meanwhile, romance seems to be blossoming for Madge and Gastric, and Willis finally gets away from his mother to go on a date with Ruby. David Jason, Stephanie Cole, Tim Healy and Johnny Vegas star(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Keanu becomes increasingly frustrated with Mel's involvement and admits his concerns to Lisa. Mel's plan begins to unravel and she goes to desperate lengths to ensure she still gets what she wants. Ben lets slip to Karen that Louise and Keanu are moving to Portugal, leaving her devastated. Adam shows his true colours, unaware Mariam is listening to every word he says, leading Habiba and Iqra to hatch a plan to cheer their grandmother up. A furious Tiffany confronts Dotty over her actions(n)

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Jo Brand assumes the role of host as panellists poke fun at the week's events. Comedian Zoe Lyons and columnist, literary editor and drama critic Fintan O'Toole bolster the efforts of regular team leaders Ian Hislop and Paul Merton(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Actors and comedians Liz Carr and Miles Jupp, actor and writer Samson Kayo and broadcaster Anneka Rice.join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As ever, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory(n)

programme.title: The Blame Game
programme.desc: The satirical show poking fun at the week's news returns as comedians Tim McGarry, Neil Delamere, Jake O'Kane, Colin Murphy and guest Marcus Brigstocke give their comedy take on the long-running Brexit 'tug-o-war' and, given the time of year, the tricks that might yet turn into treats(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: She's delighted family audiences as Mary Poppins and The Sound of Music's Maria, yet retained the power to shock by appearing in the likes of 10 and A Touch of Adultery. Now singer and actress Julie Andrews is taking stock of her life in Home Work: A Memoir of My Hollywood Years, a follow-up to her critically acclaimed memoir, Home. Also appearing are Hollywood stars Reese Witherspoon and Jennifer Aniston, both starring in comedy drama series The Morning Show, and Ian McKellen, who is playing a conman in drama thriller The Good Liar. Plus, Dua Lipa performs her new single Don't Start Now(n)

programme.title: Fleabag
programme.desc: Claire and Fleabag return to their father's home for an annual family gathering to mark the anniversary of their mother's death - but this time there is an unwanted guest. Fleabag gets into a confrontation with her godmother, who reveals her plans for a sexually charged art exhibition. Comedy, written by and starring Phoebe Waller-Bridge(n)

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: For this week's task, the candidates head to the historic university cities of Oxford and Cambridge to procure nine items that are associated with both places. The added difficulty for the candidates is that they are given no clues as to which of the cities the various items are located in. In Oxford, timewasting leads to a baffling back-and-forth for one team, while in Cambridge, the other team gamble with their negotiations. Back in the boardroom, one of the contestants leaves the competition(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Gennaro Contaldo and Shivi Ramoutar, and special guest Michael Ball. Plus, archive clips featuring Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, and Nigel Slater(n)

programme.title: Flour Power
programme.desc: Four workmates at the Bruichladdich whisky distillery on Islay compete for the Flour Power Trophy. Cask master Jay, housekeeper Katie and tour guide Dave are joined by client manager Mary. Local head chef Stephen Crichton joins series judge Nicola Reith to decide on the winner, with plenty of fighting 'spirit' needed from the four whisky wizards as they create their signature bakes(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Aston Villa v Liverpool and Manchester City v Southampton in the Premier League(n)

programme.title: International Rugby League
programme.desc: New Zealand v Great Britain. Mark Chapman presents action from the second Test in a four-match tour for the Lions, which took place at Eden Park in Auckland. With commentary by Dave Woods and Brian Noble(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from a recycling centre in Witley, Surrey, with Kev Paxton and Anthony Devine helping her transform a cooker hood, a sofa and a collection of jars(n)

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at life on each of the seven continents, revealing how each distinct land mass has shaped the creatures found there and the many challenges faced by animals in a modern world dominated by humans. He begins with Antarctica, where Weddell seals grind back the ice with their teeth, colourful starfish carpet the seabed, huge colonies of king penguins crowd any ice-free land, and four-ton elephant seals fight for territory on the beach. However, the ocean here is warming - and with that comes an uncertain future(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: Final Score from Northern Ireland
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's action along with the classified results(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys ushers comedian Joe Pasquale, Newsround presenter Ricky Boleto, singer Toyah Willcox and Olympic sprinter Jeanette Kwakye to the black chair to be put to the test on their chosen specialist subjects, which are werewolf films, number one singles of the 1990s, Boudica, and Disney animation respectively. The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions in a bid to carry off the commemorative trophy(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special Halloween edition of the general knowledge quiz, with contestants Richard Blackwood, Joe Swash, Sam Nixon, Mark Rhodes, Yvette Fielding, Timmy Mallett, Anne Diamond and Kate Lawler(n)

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present the seventh live episode of this year's series, as the 10 remaining celebrity couples take to the dancefloor, hoping to impress the judges and the viewers at home. Motsi Mabuse, Craig Revel Horwood, Shirley Ballas and Bruno Tonioli will score the performances out of 10 before the voting lines open - giving viewers at home the chance to have their say and determine who will be leaving the competition(n)

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In the fourth episode, Hannah and Geoff, a couple from London, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jade is faced with a dilemma after a chance encounter leaves her feeling heartbroken. Meanwhile, Ethan regrets his decisions when Effie is rushed into hospital and he has to open up to Theo about the lies he told so she would not be refused the drug trial. Elsewhere, Danny's impromptu visit to his childhood home does not go to plan(n)

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: He is one of the most controversial figures to have sat in the Speaker's chair in the House of Commons. As John Bercow bows out after ten controversial years as Speaker, BBC Parliament revisits the career of a modernising but polarising figure. See the teenage Bercow's ‘Question Time' debut, not from the Commons but on the TV programme. Relive the former Tory MP's clashes with ministers as he's accused of bias and of bending the rules to try to stop Brexit. programme.desc: He is one of the most controversial figures to have sat in the Speaker's chair in the House of Commons. As John Bercow bows out after ten controversial years as Speaker, BBC Parliament revisits the career of a modernising but polarising figure. programme.desc: The documentary about the long-running bus route brings things up to date by looking at the service as it is today and following 24-year-old Sean Gallagher as he makes the journey from Arranmore Island to Glasgow. Singer Daniel O Donnell also shares his memories of using the bus(n)

programme.title: Binneas

programme.desc: Following gardener Heather Dewar over a year of planting, weeding and cooking. Against the backdrop of the stunning Islay countryside, she shares her horticultural knowledge and favourite recipes. In the first of two programmes, she focuses on spring and summer produce(n)

programme.title: HMS Timbertown
programme.desc: Documentary revealing the experiences of 102 sailors from Lewis who were interned in a PoW camp in the Netherlands for the duration of the First World War. Presenter Angela MacLean retraces the journey the men took to the camp, which became known as HMS Timbertown, and uncovers the realities of their lives there - from the highs of football matches, to the lows of failed escape attempts and execution(n)

programme.title: Trapped
programme.desc: With no police support able to arrive from Reykjavik until the snowstorm clears, Andri and his colleagues need to work fast. programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features(n)

programme.title: RWC 2019 Live
programme.desc: New Zealand v Wales (Kick-off 9.00am). Coverage of the bronze final at Tokyo Stadium, where the losing semi-finalists face each other. Reigning champions New Zealand saw their quest for a third consecutive crown brought to an end following 19-7 loss to England, while Wales suffered the heartache of a narrow 19-16 defeat to South Africa(n)

programme.title: Countrywise
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin visits the tiny island of Lunga off the south-west coast of Scotland for an encounter with a colony of Atlantic Puffins, while Ben Fogle travels to Devon to the cowboys who drive their cattle across the tough terrain of Dartmoor each year the old fashioned way. Plus, a trip to Dorset with musician and actor Martin Kemp for a crabbing excursion with the local fishermen and a trip down memory lane(n)

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Guest panellists engage in topical debate and celebrity interviews from a female perspective(n)

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: Belper in Derbyshire is host to David Dickinson and his dealers. Stewart Hofgartner plays detective over a mystery item, David Tupman sets his sights on a Chinese vase, and a battle ensues for one lucky seller's medals at auction(n)

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: A team of five family members answers questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then tries to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Hosted by Warwick Davis(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Andy, Jackie, Matt and Vivien work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Someone receives some life changing news. Meanwhile, Rhona puts Marlon in his place(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jealous that Maria and Gary have arranged a date, Ali dismisses Emma's pain as food poisoning, but it turns out to be far more serious. Gemma is worried when she is told she is being discharged but the babies must remain at the hospital in Llandudno(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the discovery of an unidentified teenage boy's body in a coastal town's shipyard, who is revealed to be listed as a missing person, having absconded from the care of his support worker. Vera discovers that the quiet community where he was found is plagued by a serious drugs problem - despite the fact that the only known dealer in the area has turned his back on crime. The victim could be the answer to the mystery, but a wall of silence surrounds his death. Mark Addy guest stars(n)

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Against Murtaugh's advice, Cole reaches out to Tom Barnes for help with a case that hits close to home, thus putting the duo's partnership put to the test. Meanwhile, Trish prepares to announce her campaign for district attorney. Damon Wayans, Seann William Scott and Keesha Sharp star(n)

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness presents the dating show, with a stripper, a farmer and a gymnast and Scarlett Moffatt's ex-boyfriend entering the 'love lift', and giving it their all in the hope of impressing 30 single women and winning a date. But they must first get the female participants to keep their lights on as a sign of approval(n)

programme.title: Rugby World Cup 2019 Highlights
programme.desc: New Zealand v Wales. Jill Douglas introduces action from the third-place play-off at Tokyo Stadium, where the losing semi-finalists were aiming to end on a high(n)

programme.title: Meet the Parents
programme.desc: Dating show, hosted by Holly Willoughby, which puts the love lives of singletons in the hands of the people they know best - their mum and dad. Each week, one man and one woman must choose from three potential partners, with their decisions solely based on an encounter with the contestants' parents(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Super 4

programme.title: Super 4
programme.desc: Rock Brock becomes the target of an angry elf after he breaks his magic cauldron during a battle with a ferocious dragon - or so he says(n)

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: The heroes of International Rescue must deal with emergencies on two fronts when an orbiting solar energy station malfunctions, placing a passing airliner - and its precious cargo - in peril(n)

programme.title: RWC 2019 Live
programme.desc: Mark Pougatch is joined by a panel of experts to look at the latest team news and make predictions ahead of the World Cup final(n)

programme.title: RWC 2019 Live
programme.desc: England v South Africa (Kick-off 9.00am). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the showpiece match, which takes place at International Stadium Yokohama and will see the winners of the Webb Ellis Cup crowned. Eddie Jones' men reached their first World Cup final since 2007 with a thrilling 19-7 victory over reigning champions New Zealand seven days ago, while the Springboks overcame Wales in a more attritional affair on Sunday. With commentary by Nick Mullins(n)

programme.title: Riding a Dream
programme.desc: The remarkable story of 18-year-old British Muslim woman Khadijah Mellah, and her journey to the 2019 Magnolia Cup. From her inner city riding school to one of the most prestigious horse racing events in the world, this film follows her ups and downs as she gets ready for the race of her life(n)

programme.title: Ninja Warrior UK
programme.desc: Competitors from across the nation test their strength, speed and courage in the semi-final of the obstacle-course challenge, hosted by Ben Shephard, Rochelle Humes and Chris Kamara. Those who make it to the end of the adrenaline-fuelled mission face one final task - scaling the heights of a tower named Mount Midoriyama - as they battle it out for the title of Ninja Warrior UK. With brand new obstacles to face, the course is more extreme than ever, as the best competitors from four heats return to fight it out for a place in the final(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring themes of sport, gardens, birthdays, bad animals and sibling and including everything from giddy grandmas, cute kids and crazy animals to below-the-belt mishaps, practical jokers and painful-looking accidents(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The apparent suicide of a childless farmer's wife puts Barnaby on the scent of an ancient land feud over Setwale Wood. Potions, witchcraft, marital infidelity and a ruthless campaign to save the area form the heady brew that convinces the detective he is dealing with a case of murder. Wendy Craig guest stars, with John Nettles and Daniel Casey(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Mikey North, Una Healy, Carl Froch and Alison Hammond pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: A model, a gardener, a cage fighter and Mr Luton are the latest candidates to enter the 'love lift' in the hope of impressing 30 single women and winning a date - but must first get the female participants to keep their lights on as a sign of approval. Paddy McGuinness presents(n)

programme.title: The X Factor: Celebrity
programme.desc: Dermot O'Leary hosts the next stage in the star-studded version of the singing contest, which features further live performances in London. The remaining acts have got through the initial rounds in Los Angeles and the judge's deliberation, and now must battle it out for the chance to be crowned champion and win a record deal. They will have the support and coaching of the judges, with Simon Cowell taking charge of the Groups, Nicole Scherzinger mentoring the Overs, and Louis Walsh looking after the Unders(n)

programme.desc: The host's guests this week are broadcasting icon Trevor McDonald, footballer Peter Crouch, model Jourdan Dunn and award-winning comedian Aisling Bea. Plus, singers John Newman and Nina Nesbitt will be performing together in the studio(n)

programme.title: Be Cool, Scooby-Doo!

programme.title: Robozuna
programme.desc: Children's sci-fi fantasy series about 14-year-old orphan Ariston and his robot Mangle, fighting an evil empire together(n)

programme.title: Beautiful Baking with Juliet Sear
programme.desc: Baking expert Juliet Sear whips up a selection of treats in her studio kitchen, beginning with warm crumble tart, perfect for Sunday lunch, pasta pie and the ultimate chocolate fudge cake. She is also joined by award-winning baker Julie Jones for a lesson in pie art, and she visits a meeting of the Pudding Club in the Cotswolds(n)

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: Newlyweds John Torode and Lisa Faulkner are back in their kitchen with recipes including a traditional kedgeree for breakfast, a family favourite vegetable katsu curry, spiced lamb cutlets with potatoes and a coconut and mango rice pudding for dessert(n)

programme.title: Nadia's Family Feasts
programme.desc: Nadia Sawalha presents recipe ideas for the week ahead based on different cuisines from around the world, beginning with the tastes of Italy. Leading chef Masha Rener joins her to help prepare a full day's menu - sausage and bean ragu for breakfast, Italian herb-roasted chicken for lunch, Masha's mezzeluna for dinner and Nadia's tasty tiramisu for dessert(n)

programme.title: About a Boy (2002)
programme.desc: An idle womaniser pretends to be a father to meet women in a single mothers' group - but he starts to change his selfish ways when a lonely youngster with a depressed mum becomes emotionally attached to him. Romantic comedy adapted from Nick Hornby's novel, starring Hugh Grant, Toni Collette, Nicholas Hoult and Rachel Weisz(n)

programme.title: The X Factor: Celebrity
programme.desc: Dermot O'Leary hosts the next stage in the star-studded version of the singing contest, which features further live performances in London. The remaining acts have got through the initial rounds in Los Angeles and the judge's deliberation, and now must battle it out for the chance to be crowned champion and win a record deal. They will have the support and coaching of the judges, with Simon Cowell taking charge of the Groups, Nicole Scherzinger mentoring the Overs, and Louis Walsh looking after the Unders(n)

programme.title: Back to Mine
programme.desc: Jasmine from Suffolk will not settle for romance unless it's amazing. She wants a man with a passion for life and with plenty of interests - and he absolutely must love animals. In an attempt to find Mr Right, she looks round the homes of three potential partners before choosing one for a date - but will any give a clue to the kind of man she is after?(n)

programme.title: Harry Potter and the Order of the Phoenix (2007)
programme.desc: The young wizard struggles to convince his fellow sorcerers that the evil Lord Voldemort has returned to life. When Hogwarts headmaster Dumbledore is ousted in favour of a sadistic replacement, Harry realises he must take matters into his own hands, uniting his fellow pupils to form a secret army and training them for the inevitable confrontation to come. Fantasy adventure sequel, starring Daniel Radcliffe, Emma Watson, Rupert Grint, Gary Oldman, Michael Gambon and Alan Rickman(n)

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show, asking actor Tim Healy, Towie star-turned-presenter Mark Wright and Coronation Street's Alexandra Mardell (Emma Brooker) to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. One of them will make it through to the Super Catchphrase round, where they could win £50,000 for the charity of their choice(n)

programme.title: David Walliams Presents - Return of Alright on the Night
programme.desc: The Britain's Got Talent judge narrates a collection of classic out-takes and bloopers in which things did not go to plan during filming(n)

programme.title: Prince Charles: Inside the Duchy of Cornwall
programme.desc: Part two of two. The documentary examines Charles's legacy for the Duchy, as senior staff prepare William for the day that he succeeds his father as the next Duke of Cornwall. The documentary also examines his well-known passion for trees and traditional rural crafts, and how these may shape the Duchy's future(n)

programme.title: Paul O'Grady: For the Love of Dogs
programme.desc: The comedian follows the work of Battersea Dogs & Cats Home in London, meeting staff involved in treating, training and rehoming animals. This time, Paul meets a 10-month-old puppy who has been brought into Battersea twice for being too boisterous, before helping to soothe a pointer cross who has had a hard life(n)

programme.title: Holiday Horrors: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Documentary series looking at what can go wrong on holiday, including a ski trip that became a fight for survival, a horrific case of sunburn, and what happened when a wildfire spread across the holiday hotspot of Javea in Spain. Presented by Jamie Theakston(n)

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features(n)

programme.title: RWC 2019 Live
programme.desc: New Zealand v Wales (Kick-off 9.00am). Coverage of the bronze final at Tokyo Stadium, where the losing semi-finalists face each other. Reigning champions New Zealand saw their quest for a third consecutive crown brought to an end following 19-7 loss to England, while Wales suffered the heartache of a narrow 19-16 defeat to South Africa(n)

programme.title: Countrywise
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin visits the tiny island of Lunga off the south-west coast of Scotland for an encounter with a colony of Atlantic Puffins, while Ben Fogle travels to Devon to the cowboys who drive their cattle across the tough terrain of Dartmoor each year the old fashioned way. Plus, a trip to Dorset with musician and actor Martin Kemp for a crabbing excursion with the local fishermen and a trip down memory lane(n)

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Guest panellists engage in topical debate and celebrity interviews from a female perspective(n)

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal

programme.title: Harry Potter and the Order of the Phoenix (2007)
programme.desc: The young wizard struggles to convince his fellow sorcerers that the evil Lord Voldemort has returned to life. When Hogwarts headmaster Dumbledore is ousted in favour of a sadistic replacement, Harry realises he must take matters into his own hands, uniting his fellow pupils to form a secret army and training them for the inevitable confrontation to come. Fantasy adventure sequel, starring Daniel Radcliffe, Emma Watson, Rupert Grint, Gary Oldman, Michael Gambon and Alan Rickman(n)

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show, asking actor Tim Healy, Towie star-turned-presenter Mark Wright and Coronation Street's Alexandra Mardell (Emma Brooker) to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. One of them will make it through to the Super Catchphrase round, where they could win £50,000 for the charity of their choice(n)

programme.title: David Walliams Presents - Return of Alright on the Night
programme.desc: The Britain's Got Talent judge narrates a collection of classic out-takes and bloopers in which things did not go to plan during filming(n)

programme.title: Prince Charles: Inside the Duchy of Cornwall
programme.desc: Part two of two. The documentary examines Charles's legacy for the Duchy, as senior staff prepare William for the day that he succeeds his father as the next Duke of Cornwall. The documentary also examines his well-known passion for trees and traditional rural crafts, and how these may shape the Duchy's future(n)

programme.desc: Steve and Snot are humiliated when they attend a 'cool kids' party. programme.title: The Cleveland Show

programme.desc: The sleuth investigates her friend Elspeth McGillicuddy's claim to have witnessed a murder being committed on a passing train during a rail journey, but everyone is stumped by the lack of a body. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, Pam Ferris, Niamh Cusack, John Hannah, Amanda Holden, Griff Rhys Jones, David Warner, Jenny Agutter and Rob Brydon(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: A local woman is killed when an experiment in biological warfare goes tragically wrong. Sam also falls victim to a potentially fatal illness as a result of the project. Foyle is forced to head into the most secretive and dangerous areas of wartime research, unearthing secrets that cannot be revealed even to the prime minister. Michael Kitchen stars, with Honeysuckle Weeks and Philip Franks(n)

programme.title: The Street
programme.desc: Joe Jennerson fails to resist taking on the role of his identical twin brother Harry when he chokes on a sweet and dies. Stepping into the shoes of his sibling, Joe leaves behind his wife and dead-end job, and settles into a comfortable, bachelor lifestyle - but knows he cannot keep up his pretence for ever. Starring Timothy Spall and David Thewlis(n)

programme.title: The Street
programme.desc: Eddie agrees to attend a charity auction in a bid to impress his first love Pat - now married to his old friend Bob. His wife Margie initially agrees to go along, but decides to forego the event when she discovers a lump in her breast. A night of big spending and heavy drinking marks the start of a chain of unfortunate events that leave Eddie and his wife looking to each other for support. Timothy Spall and Ger Ryan star(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Poirot visits a garden fete where he meets the son of an old friend and his fiancee. After having tea with the couple, the sleuth suspects that they are both in danger and Hastings' new interest in photography provides him with the clue to solve a mystery involving cyanide, failing brakes and mixed-up love lives.(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Following the loss of their funding, the residents are in dire financial straits. Danny and Ed are refused an extension on their mortgage, and an attempt to solve the family's problems once and for all backfires dramatically. Later, Danny breaks news about his future that leaves Du Plessis devastated. African-set wildlife drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, Robert Bathurst and Deon Stewardson(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Strange things are happening at Leopard's Den as Danny prepares to leave for the UK. Frogs have seemingly rained down overnight, the giraffe refuses to budge from the front gate and the cheetah has lost its appetite. Danny jumps at the chance to have one last adventure before his return home - tracking a lioness that attacked a tourist - but it could end in tragedy. Meanwhile, Du Plessis makes an alarming discovery about Ed and Fiona(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The glamorous Ruth Van Rydock contacts her old friend Jane Marple with a mystery - her sister Carrie Louise's study has been devastated by a suspicious fire. While Carrie Louise has the same cheerful outlook as ever, Ruth fears dark forces are at play at Stonygates, the home Carrie shares with her third husband, various relatives and a motley crew of young criminals, the latest subject of Carrie's philanthropic endeavours. But on the evening before a theatrical show is staged for their guests, one of whom is Miss Marple, an even more fascinating performance takes place before their very eyes - one where the final act is murder. Julia McKenzie stars, with guest appearances from Joan Collins, Penelope Wilton, Brian Cox, Maxine Peake, Alexei Sayle, Sarah Smart, Ian Ogilvy, Sean Hughes and Nigel Terry(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A man accused of murdering five people is killed in prison shortly before going to trial, wrapping up a lot of loose ends and saving the state the expense of keeping him behind bars indefinitely. Alas, Morse cannot help wondering why the body of one of his victims was never found - and the chance discovery of a corpse gives him the perfect opportunity to investigate further. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A young woman asks Poirot to investigate her father's murder - a 14-year-old crime for which her mother was found guilty and executed. The detective agrees to investigate, but warns his client that he may discover things she does not want to hear. David Suchet stars, with Rachael Stirling, Aidan Gillen, Aimee Mullens, Toby Stephens, Gemma Jones, Patrick Malahide and Annette Badland(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates a double poisoning, aiming to prove the innocence of Elinor Carlisle, the chief suspect in the deaths of her aunt and her rival in love. However, it emerges that she is the only person with a motive, opportunity and means to administer the poison. Crime drama, starring David Suchet, Elisabeth Dermot-Walsh, Diana Quick, Paul McGann and Rupert Penry-Jones(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A philandering husband is found dying by the swimming pool after being shot during a house party at the home of Lady Lucy Angkatell. Legendary Belgian Sleuth Poirot, who is guest of honour at the gathering, appears to have an open-and-shut case on his hands - but the investigator finds himself up against one of his finest adversaries. Whodunit, guest-starring Sarah Miles (Ryan's Daughter), with David Suchet, Lysette Anthony and Edward Fox(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A professor feels humiliated when her internet dating video is leaked onto a website and watched by her students. The next morning she is found dead, the victim of what appears to be a straightforward suicide. However, Lewis is not convinced and digs deeper, identifying several suspects including the site's founder - but as he and Hathaway get closer to the truth amid a rising death toll, they find their personal and professional lives dredged up online for all to see. Guest starring Roxanne McKee (Game of Thrones) and Toby Stephens (Die Another Day), with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: The detective has been assigned to a quiet rural area in 1969, and finds himself back in uniform. However, he is reunited with his former CID colleagues when he discovers the body of a missing schoolgirl. Endeavour quickly finds a likely suspect, and is offered the chance to regain his lost status by making an arrest - but Thursday convinces him the man is innocent(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost

Things take a shocking turn when the car finally turns up - with the body of a drug dealer in the boot. Detective drama, starring David Jason, with Melanie Thaw and Bruce Alexander(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A lavish dinner party proves to be a prelude to murder and intrigue - but fortunately Poirot is among the guests, and soon realises the key to the mystery lies in a duel fought 10 years previously. Drama, starring David Suchet, Caroline Langrishe and John McEnery(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Hollywood actress Marina Gregg and her director husband take up residence at Gossington Hall in the village of St Mary Mead and hold a reception to meet their new neighbours, including the hall's previous owner Dolly Bantry. The celebrations turn to tragedy, however, after a fan fatally drinks a poisoned cocktail apparently meant for the new lady of the manor. But a different picture begins to emerge as Marina spits out a similarly spiked cup of coffee on the set of her new film - and Miss Marple investigates the star's unhappy past. Julia McKenzie stars, with Lindsay Duncan, Nigel Harman, Joanna Lumley, Martin Jarvis, Hugh Bonneville and Michele Dotrice(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army (2016)
programme.desc: The officious captain of a seaside town's home guard platoon tries to maintain the reputation and morale of his troops as the Second World War draws to a close. He finds himself dealing with the hunt for a German spy as well as a female journalist sent to report on the exploits of the brave but bumbling soldiers. Comedy based on the TV series, starring Toby Jones, Bill Nighy and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A depressive gambler is found dead, the apparent victim of suicide. Dr Jane Moore, who once treated the man, is adamant he wouldn't have taken his own life - a theory backed up by the post-mortem. Thrilled to be working with Jane again, Barnaby investigates the case - and it isn't long before another of the doctor's patients is killed. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, Isla Blair, Jan Ravens and Daniel Casey(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: An idyllic summer day in Oxford is torn asunder when a parcel bomb claims the life of eminent mathematician Adam Capstone, a genius in the field of 'Knot Theory'. As Lewis and Hathaway investigate, they discover that, while the victim's academic reputation was beyond reproach, his private life was far more dubious. It emerges Capstone was a man of carnal appetites, who had a string of affairs with young students, betraying his chemistry professor wife Elizabeth, providing her with a clear motive to do away with her cheating spouse. As the detectives work to identify the killer bomber, the case becomes a personal challenge for Lewis, who fears he may not be rewarded with another contract should he fail to impress Moody before his six-month trip to New Zealand. Detective drama, starring Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox, with Zoe Tapper, Tristam Summers and Oliver Lansley(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.title: On the Buses

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: A nostalgic Stan remembers his first day at the depot, when he was a conductor and Blakey was a driver. He also recounts the events leading to Arthur and Olive's engagement. Comedy, starring Reg Varney, Stephen Lewis and Michael Robbins(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Mr Roper plans to convert the flats into smaller dwelling units - but he has not bargained for Robin, Chrissy and Jo having a three-year lease. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian sleuth goes on an Egyptian cruise on board an old steamer, but when an American heiress is murdered, his little grey cells go into overdrive. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates her friend Elspeth McGillicuddy's claim to have witnessed a murder being committed on a passing train during a rail journey, but everyone is stumped by the lack of a body. Michael Kitchen stars, with Honeysuckle Weeks and Philip Franks(n)

programme.title: The Street
programme.desc: Joe Jennerson fails to resist taking on the role of his identical twin brother Harry when he chokes on a sweet and dies. Stepping into the shoes of his sibling, Joe leaves behind his wife and dead-end job, and settles into a comfortable, bachelor lifestyle - but knows he cannot keep up his pretence for ever. Starring Timothy Spall and David Thewlis(n)

programme.title: The Street
programme.desc: Eddie agrees to attend a charity auction in a bid to impress his first love Pat - now married to his old friend Bob. His wife Margie initially agrees to go along, but decides to forego the event when she discovers a lump in her breast. A night of big spending and heavy drinking marks the start of a chain of unfortunate events that leave Eddie and his wife looking to each other for support. Timothy Spall and Ger Ryan star(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Poirot visits a garden fete where he meets the son of an old friend and his fiancee. After having tea with the couple, the sleuth suspects that they are both in danger and Hastings' new interest in photography provides him with the clue to solve a mystery involving cyanide, failing brakes and mixed-up love lives.(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Following the loss of their funding, the residents are in dire financial straits. Danny and Ed are refused an extension on their mortgage, and an attempt to solve the family's problems once and for all backfires dramatically. Later, Danny breaks news about his future that leaves Du Plessis devastated. African-set wildlife drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, Robert Bathurst and Deon Stewardson(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Strange things are happening at Leopard's Den as Danny prepares to leave for the UK. Frogs have seemingly rained down overnight, the giraffe refuses to budge from the front gate and the cheetah has lost its appetite. Danny jumps at the chance to have one last adventure before his return home - tracking a lioness that attacked a tourist - but it could end in tragedy. Meanwhile, Du Plessis makes an alarming discovery about Ed and Fiona(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan gets a new uniform and is proud to wear it - until he sits on a freshly painted chair. Classic comedy, starring Reg Varney(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Arthur is attracted to Beryl, the clippie on his regular bus. However, she has only two interests in life - men with a fondness for athletics, and those with a good head of hair. Reg Varney stars(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The glamorous Ruth Van Rydock contacts her old friend Jane Marple with a mystery - her sister Carrie Louise's study has been devastated by a suspicious fire. While Carrie Louise has the same cheerful outlook as ever, Ruth fears dark forces are at play at Stonygates, the home Carrie shares with her third husband, various relatives and a motley crew of young criminals, the latest subject of Carrie's philanthropic endeavours. But on the evening before a theatrical show is staged for their guests, one of whom is Miss Marple, an even more fascinating performance takes place before their very eyes - one where the final act is murder. Julia McKenzie stars, with guest appearances from Joan Collins, Penelope Wilton, Brian Cox, Maxine Peake, Alexei Sayle, Sarah Smart, Ian Ogilvy, Sean Hughes and Nigel Terry(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A man accused of murdering five people is killed in prison shortly before going to trial, wrapping up a lot of loose ends and saving the state the expense of keeping him behind bars indefinitely. Alas, Morse cannot help wondering why the body of one of his victims was never found - and the chance discovery of a corpse gives him the perfect opportunity to investigate further. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A young woman asks Poirot to investigate her father's murder - a 14-year-old crime for which her mother was found guilty and executed. The detective agrees to investigate, but warns his client that he may discover things she does not want to hear. David Suchet stars, with Rachael Stirling, Aidan Gillen, Aimee Mullens, Toby Stephens, Gemma Jones, Patrick Malahide and Annette Badland(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates a double poisoning, aiming to prove the innocence of Elinor Carlisle, the chief suspect in the deaths of her aunt and her rival in love. However, it emerges that she is the only person with a motive, opportunity and means to administer the poison. Crime drama, starring David Suchet, Elisabeth Dermot-Walsh, Diana Quick, Paul McGann and Rupert Penry-Jones(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A philandering husband is found dying by the swimming pool after being shot during a house party at the home of Lady Lucy Angkatell. Legendary Belgian Sleuth Poirot, who is guest of honour at the gathering, appears to have an open-and-shut case on his hands - but the investigator finds himself up against one of his finest adversaries. Whodunit, guest-starring Sarah Miles (Ryan's Daughter), with David Suchet, Lysette Anthony and Edward Fox(n)

Endeavour quickly finds a likely suspect, and is offered the chance to regain his lost status by making an arrest - but Thursday convinces him the man is innocent(n)

The celebrations turn to tragedy, however, after a fan fatally drinks a poisoned cocktail apparently meant for the new lady of the manor. But a different picture begins to emerge as Marina spits out a similarly spiked cup of coffee on the set of her new film - and Miss Marple investigates the star's unhappy past. programme.desc: Dealers Celia Sawyer, Alex Proud, Raj Bisram and David Sonnenthal are enticed by a signed first edition Harry Potter book and a pair of 19th-century Japanese paintings(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Documentary revealing the secret life of the animals of Chester Zoo, one of Britain's most popular zoos, using new micro-rig technology to capture the behaviour of the animals and their close relationships with their keepers. Vets at Chester Zoo treat an elephant calf for the herpes virus, the same virus that resulted in the deaths of two calves the year before. A breeding phenomenon plays out among the extraordinary cardinal fish, and the zoo's humboldt penguins welcome 16 new members to their colony(n)

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather(n)

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits Canterbury to explore the Plantagenet era, when the powerful house held the throne between 1154 and 1485. She discovers the secrets of the city's cathedral, a centre of ecclesiastical power and also the site of the shocking murder of Thomas Becket. She also discovers the lasting impact of the Black Death and joins a modern-day pilgrimage in medieval garb. Aerial archaeologist Ben Robinson provides a bird's-eye view of how the religious institutions of the era still dominate the city's topography(n)

programme.title: Great British Car Journeys
programme.desc: Christopher Timothy and Peter Davison travel along Roman road the Fosse Way, starting in Axmouth, putting the Morgan through its paces on some rough terrain. Along the way, they take a 1930s driving test and go for a spin in one of Peter's favourite cars(n)

programme.title: Ghostbusters (2016)
programme.desc: When a series of supernatural disturbances occurs in New York City, two experts in the paranormal investigate, joined by a brilliant inventor who has created an array of ghost-hunting gadgets and a public transport worker whose knowledge of the city provides vital clues to the sinister cause of the ghostly activity. Reboot of the 1980s comedy franchise, starring Kristen Wiig, Melissa McCarthy, Kate McKinnon, Leslie Jones and Chris Hemsworth(n)

programme.title: Gogglebox

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Part one of two. Mr Burns angers the residents of Springfield with a plot to block out the sun's light, which would force the town to use the electricity generated by his power plant. programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Moe is elated to discover Homer was born with a unique genetic condition that protects his brain from injury, and decides to cash in by training him to be a boxer. Featuring the guest voice of Paul Winfield(n)

programme.title: The Dog House

programme.title: The Gadget Show
programme.desc: Members of the G Team pitch the latest survival tech to former Royal Marine sniper Aldo Kane, while Jon Bentley pounds the pavements with a TV journalist for the day to test out the latest big screen smartphones. Over in California, Georgie Barrat discovers why the electric scooter company's success hasn't yet made it this side of the pond, before Ortis Deley gets an exclusive try himself during their first UK trials. Plus, this week's Wall of Fame sees Jon pitch for the UK's best-selling computer of all time, the Raspberry Pi. Presented by Craig Charles(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Cameras capture a route through Spain, beginning near Leon, part of the famous north Spanish Pilgrimage, in the Picos de Europa mountains. The railway then runs through the Basque country before the famous Guggenheim Museum greets travellers in Bilbao. In Galicia, the gorges, coastline and sea bridges are a sight for sore eyes, and a speciality cider tour refreshes the train's guests. The journey ends in Ferrol, a spectacular harbour that has been a major shipbuilding centre for most of its history and has been a key city in many wars. Narrated by Bill Nighy(n)

programme.title: Caravanning with Shane Richie
programme.desc: The presenter visits a vast campsite in North Yorkshire, which has its own theme park and zoo. The place is so big that guests can call for a golf-buggy taxi just to take them to their room. Once, settled in, Shane hops on the cable cars for an aerial view of the park's rollercoasters and wild animal enclosures. He also explores the local area, taking in a steam train trip from inside the driver's cabin and a fishing trip out of Whitby. Following in his father's footsteps as an performer, Shane's son arrives with his band to perform as the site's entertainment(n)

programme.title: Undercover Girlfriends
programme.desc: The women confront their partners one-on-one after having meticulously planned their final showdowns, with the others watching on as each face-off with a shocked boyfriend occurs. Some couples are happily reunited, while others end on a different page altogether(n)

programme.title: The Best of Bad TV
programme.desc: The return of the documentary celebrating those moments when events on the small screen went wrong in the most spectacular ways imaginable. The highlights - or rather lowlights - include TV cook Fanny Cradock taking a competition winner to task for creating a dish that did not live up to her exacting standards, and a variety show co-starring Formula One drivers - giving James Hunt a chance to literally blow his own trumpet. There are also various talent show cock-ups, including a memorable moment when a microphone failed during Fame Academy, and plenty of soaps, music and drama disasters too, from tears in Changing Rooms to a talking dog in That's Life!(n)

programme.title: Secret Life of the Holiday Resort
programme.desc: Observational documentary lifting the lid on an all-inclusive hotel in the heart of Spain's Costa Brava, where thousands of Brits flock each year to enjoy the easy life, helping themselves to all the booze they can drink and all the food they can eat. An assortment of guests check in each week, all with different reasons for escaping to the sun. Appearing in the first episode are tattoo model Ryan and his mates from Sheffield, former military chef Ian Dean and his smooth-talking grandson Jack, and newly-engaged fast-food workers Kurt and Lizzi(n)

programme.title: 10 Mistakes That Sank the Titanic
programme.desc: Documentary examining the chain of events that led to the sinking of the ship April 14, 1912, when it struck an iceberg in the north Atlantic and went down with around 1,500 passengers and crew. From the procedure for fixing rivets into the ship's bows, the delays in its construction that pushed the maiden voyage into perilous iceberg season, and the astonishing fact that the lookouts on watch that fateful night had no access to binoculars, this film reveals the 10 key mistakes that conspired to sink the supposedly unsinkable Titanic(n)

programme.title: Then & Now
programme.desc: The series concludes with a look at the origins of the Routemaster bus, which was engineered from Second World War aircraft technology, and a look behind the scenes in the control centre that keeps transport in London running. Plus, a look back at the Kings Cross tube disaster and a look at a project that would have seen London divided by four giant motorways(n)

programme.title: Great British Ships
programme.desc: Rob Bell boards the ship that kick started the age of polar exploration as he looks at the history of RRS Discovery, the world's first purpose-built scientific vessel. Heading to the Victorian shipbuilding capital of Dundee, Rob finds out why this was the only place in Britain that could make a ship capable of taking on the formidable ice floes of Antarctica, and tells the story of how a chance meeting in London would define the course of polar exploration(n)

programme.title: Angels & Demons (2009)
programme.desc: Academic adventurer Robert Langdon is called in by the Vatican to deal with a crisis. Pope Pius XVI has died suddenly and the four cardinals in line to be his successor have all been abducted by a mysterious sect that is threatening to murder them. As he searches for the kidnapped clerics, Langdon discovers the cult has an even more horrific plan in mind involving a stolen canister of anti-matter. First up is 43-year-old Cathy from Hertfordshire who, with her biological clock ticking, is wanting to settle down and have children. The trouble is she's open to dating men from their 20s to their 60s and finds it hard to choose. Will she find it easier to pick the right partner when they're all naked? However, they cannot decide whether this is in Somerset or near Valencia in Spain. Jonnie Irwin and Laura Hamilton step in with some expert advice(n)

Guest 'Deano' Whiting misses the hustle and bustle of Blackpool and isn't impressed by the in-room extras. Meanwhile, sisters Nina and Nicola Hilton from Bleak House put their foot in it(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the second night from the competition in Edinburgh, and cafe owner Nutan is hoping her happy-go-lucky attitude will have her laughing all the way to the bank(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The third host of the week in Edinburgh is South-African born family man Sindiso, who hopes an evening of fun inspired by his homeland will win him the £1,000 prize(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Edinburgh security company manager Anne hopes her homely, relaxed night in will be enough to win the £1,000 prize - but rising tensions between the guests may scupper her chances(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the final night in Edinburgh, and pilot James is hoping a touch of sophistication in his high-flying menu will see him land safely in first place. However, after the previous night's tension, his main concern is keeping any potential explosions contained. Guests Anne and Larah have a suspicion he could be serious competition. Will James manage to produce excellent food and keep the peace, leading him to victory?(n)

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: In Brixworth, Northamptonshire, rugby fan Ian wants to leave the property that he and wife Sarah bought together two years ago. With a toddler and a couple of dogs, their two-bed rural semi is bursting at the seams. However, Sarah believes the house has the potential to cater for a growing family and wants to stay - if they can sort out their dingy ground floor. Kirstie wants to open up the downstairs and drive a digger through the conservatory, but will Ian and Sarah like the idea?(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A Labrador is rushed to Fitzpatrick's unable to stand, and although Professor Noel Fitzpatrick tells the family that surgery is not an option, his team devises an alternative care plan. The vets also treat a bichon frise suffering chronic elbow pain, and a leonberger has a bone tumour removed from its leg after being diagnosed with cancer(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets a master craftsman whose dream is to build a castle in rural Devon made entirely of mud. Kevin McCabe plans to form the huge property out of two vast curved roundhouses - the largest of which is inspired by the natural geometry of a snail shell - connected by glazing and topped with wildflower meadow roofs to mirror the surrounding countryside. However, the project involves mixing and laying a mind-boggling 2,000 tons of cob, all during warm, sunny weather - and the sheer scale of the task soon becomes clear(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: The wedding season draws to close, but Dick and Angel need to look ahead to the winter. Dick ploughs up the wild flower meadow, a change that Angel does not entirely approve of, and the couple transforms the dilapidated coach house into a cafe, complete with a striking neon sign(n)

programme.title: The Team
programme.desc: Seven people are murdered during a brutal shootout at a remote bed and breakfast. The only survivor of the massacre is a young Syrian woman, who escapes into the vast surrounding marshland. The victims of Marsh Farm also include some illegal migrants who had evidently gone into hiding there. A student from Brussels who had previously been in contact with the Caliphate Union terrorist organisation and acted as an informer for the Belgian secret service, is also killed during the attack. Inspector Nelly Winther from Denmark, Hamburg investigator Gregor Weiss and Belgian secret service officer Paula Liekens spring into action to find those responsible. Crime thriller, starring Jurgen Vogel, Marie Bach Hansen and Lynn Van Royen. In English, German, Flemish, French and Danish(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: In an edition of the documentary focusing on fathers and sons, 51-year-old builder Rod is rushed into A&E after falling 18 feet from scaffolding onto concrete. When scans reveal that he has fractured some of his vertebrae and will require extensive surgery, his wife Nicky is left to call their two sons to prepare them for bad news. Meanwhile, 86-year-old retired policeman John is brought to St George's with a head injury after falling off a wall. As doctors assess the severity of the damage, he talks about how his career took over his life, so much so that he missed the birth of his son. Plus, 19-year-old cyclist Mack requires specialist help for a dislocated shoulder, after he collided with a Porsche. His dad explains how adopting him and his brother changed all of their lives(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Three-year-old Louie is airlifted to St George's after being run over by a car while playing outside, with doctors expressing concern about his neck, which seems to be stuck in one position. Elsewhere, 14-year-old Josh is treated after injuring his ankle when riding a BMX with his father on the front. Meanwhile, 41-year-old Sam arrives in resus with a cut to her head and possible spinal and internal injuries after losing control of her car and crashing into a tree(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
